text: During a laparoscopic appendectomy for retrocecal retroperitoneal appendicitis, the surgeon uses a hook to dissect lateral peritoneal cecal adhesions. Maryland forceps are employed for blunt dissection of the retrocecal fascia, gradually revealing the appendix. Further mobilization and blunt dissection allow the surgeon to grasp the appendix for continued dissection.

The surgeon identifies the mesoappendix and performs further blunt dissection. The lateral peritoneal reflection is cut, and a hook is used for additional dissection of the lateral peritoneal reflection.
The surgeon seals the mesoappendix using monopolar dissection, then uses a hook to elevate and cut the mesoappendix, creating a visible window in the tissue.

The surgeon uses a hook to separate the remaining adhesions around the tip of the appendix. The base of the appendix is identified and skeletalized, followed by ligation using a ligature. The base is further secured with an additional ligature before the appendix is cut.
